subject: Virology
title: Shingles: The Zoster Decode
hook: Shingles is one fact away from a wrong answer. Here is the zoster story examiners test.

points.heading: One virus, two diseases
points.body: Primary VZV infection causes chickenpox in children. Reactivation years later causes herpes zoster, or shingles, driven by a decline in cell-mediated immunity. Most patients are over 50.

points.heading: Latency in the dorsal root ganglia
points.body: After chickenpox resolves, VZV stays dormant in the dorsal root ganglia. On reactivation it travels down a single sensory nerve, which is why zoster is unilateral and dermatomal.

points.heading: Pain comes before the rash
points.body: A pre-eruptive phase of dermatomal pain or paraesthesia precedes the vesicles by 2 to 3 days. The rash is grouped vesicles on an erythematous base. Zoster sine herpete is dermatomal pain with no rash.

points.heading: PHN: know the cutoff
points.body: Post-herpetic neuralgia is pain persisting more than one month after the infection. Antivirals started within 72 hours of symptoms shorten the attack and reduce PHN, so timing is everything.

caption: Shingles is a guaranteed exam topic, and it rewards precision: dorsal root ganglia latency, a unilateral dermatomal rash, PHN at more than one month, and the 72-hour antiviral window.
